title: ferray-ufunc: no integer SIMD paths — all integer ops are scalar
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

CLAUDE.md says 'All contiguous inner loops must have SIMD paths for f32, f64, i32, i64'. The actual codebase only has SIMD paths for f64 (and partial f32). Integer operations (add, sub, mul, bitwise, shift, comparison) all go through the generic iter().map().collect() path with no SIMD. NumPy has SIMD-dispatched loops for integer types via loops_arithmetic.dispatch.c.src. pulp supports integer SIMD (i32x4, i64x2, etc.) — these should be used for the core integer arithmetic and bitwise operations.
